rationale.R1.6: The delegation is unambiguously a writing-polish request. The artifact exhibits run-on sentences, stacked hedges ('may potentially', 'possibly could'), repetitive syntactic frames ('which represents a modest improvement' ×3), and bloated qualifier chains. Sentence- and paragraph-level remediation is the entire load-bearing task.
rationale.R1.2: Peripheral only: basic logical sequencing (specific results → efficiency caveat → stability) may be lightly tidied as part of polishing paragraph flow, but no structural logic audit was requested.
rationale.R1.5: Peripheral only: the repetitive tripartite SST-2/IMDB/Yelp block may be consolidated as a by-product of prose tightening, but wholesale section reorganization is outside a 'polish' scope.
rationale.RX.1: The artifact contains several genuinely uncertain claims (efficiency, hardware dependence, 'preliminary' benchmarking). Polish must preserve or sharpen — not erase — those epistemic qualifiers.
rationale.RX.2: Heavy hedging coexists with implicit generality claims ('across sentiment classification settings'). Polishing must not inadvertently strengthen claims beyond what the data support.
rationale.RX.3: Scope is writing quality only; the agent must not introduce new empirical claims, reframe the contribution, or alter numerical values.
rationale.RX.4: Accuracy numbers and baseline comparisons are cited; any inadvertent modification could mislead readers or downstream work.
rationale.RX.5: All specific figures (92.4%, 91.8%, BERT-base comparisons) must be passed through verbatim; no silent substitution.
